content: Lexi, Veq, Yutra, and now Zyelle are all atop the Waterfall Serenity. In the distance, their friends are marched, shackled and bruised, but not beaten. At the head of the procession are Six Individuals of Terrifying Notoriety. Grand Inquisitor Vlad leads the procession himself. Vlad bears a striking resemblance to the vampire lord he's named after in mankind's lore. Pale skin, sharp features, piercing red eyes, and an aristocratic bearing that masks his inner darkness. He favors dark, flowing robes over traditional Inquisitor armor, though he retains the segmented gauntlets and a stylized helmet with a bat-like motif. Vlad carries a cane that conceals a lightsaber blade, a symbol of his authority and a weapon he wields with chilling grace.

Lexi watches the prossesion momentarily and upon spotting Zess realizes that these were indeed members of the Nighthawks. She doesn't say anything as she descends from the top of the waterfall in nearly a single leap, using the Force to catch herself in a half kneel before springing up to run into the camp with the men. "**They're moving the Nighthawks to the Interrogation suites. We need to move. Get into the vents and head towards the access by the park. Stay hidden, but be ready to extract the Nighthawks.**"

The entire camp gets mobilized immediately. Bored soldiers daydreaming lazily at the park transformed into dutiful patriots running to their tents for their helms and armaments. Even the Ysalamiri caught the spirit of the command, booming out loud and clear from the Jedi flying past them after a telekinetically empowered leap, leaving only blustering wind in her wake. This blue Force power was not of the Verdant Force, but was one originating from Lexi's pure Soul. It radiated confidence and optimism.

That optimism would be sorely tested this day. After 4 hours of rest and relaxation, Team Charlie was completely refreshed recharged and its morale restored, with the triumphant return of Stormtrooper Commander Bishop, in addition to two new utterly bucksome team members that had the entire Squad in a critical case of blue balls.

Bored lizards began crawling off warm rocks and back into their habitats. Pretty soon the entire Squad was assembled at attention. Executing a crisp salute, Bishop announced: **"White Doves. Operation Rescue Nighthawks Begins Now. Double time down the Ventilation Shaft!"**

"**Magnet, ready the comm jammers for when the conflict starts.**" With the squad well on their way to provide support, Lexi turns her attention to her small cadre of Force using allies. "**Zyelle, you and I will play it like before. Veq. Yutra. You'll stay in reserve. We'll draw them in.**"
**"Copy. I know a good hiding spot, follow me Dark Lady."**
**"You pulled it off once... Why not twice? OK lets see how this plays out from the other side..."**
**"I should be starring in holodramas after my last performance... It's gonna be hard to outdo myself from *before* lunch."**
"**May the Force be with us,**" Lexi says fervently, almost a prayer before she turns to head towards the park arena.

**"Mistress Zyelle, we're all counting on your charm to carry us through. Same for your leadership, Mistress Ardena."** Closing his fists over one another, he sprouts grasshopper wings and picks up ex-Inquisitor Yutra in his arms, flying off in the direction of the arena.

It's a bit of a close call, but Lexi and Zyelle emerge from the Ventilation Hatch once again, just in time to see the Inquisitorial Procession approach the walkway bridge that surrounds the Sparring Arena. Gaining the bridge in a series of Force-flips, Zyelle and Lexi are just able to beat the Inquisitors to the top of the ramp. The whole Cabal stops in their tracks, looking up the steps to see two defiant females. Lexi had changed hurriedly into Scientist robes, trying desperately to effect an aire of bookwormishness. The jostled hair and unkemptness only added to the illusion, thankfully.

Lexi pauses a moment as the groups meet and she swipes away a stand of hair from her face. "**Evening, Grand Inquisitor,**" she starts by addressing the Inquisitor who was obviously the leader. Pausing a moment, she takes a moment to look behind the Inquisitor as if finding the spectacle of the procession interesting. As their attention is drawn to her, she draws in on her presence to hide herself in an effort to prolong them inevitably sensing her in the Force. "**I was actually on my way to your block to evaluate prisoners. But I see now you're going to be quite busy.**"

The Grand Inquisitor had been glaring at the duo of females with unrestrained contempt until now, however his severe expression seemed to soften at Lexi's proclamation. Looking betwixt the two Light Siders, Lexi could see the Grand Inquisitor make some quick judgements behind his glowing red eyes. The arrogant Creature was smiling predatorily: one could readily sense he possessed a cold, calculating intellect. He speaks in a very rare accent, as if descending from nobility of vampires immemorial. Perhaps he was, or perhaps it was all just a ruse. Parting his scarlet lips, to reveal two vampiric incisors he intones smoothly: **"Were you now?"** He twirls his intricately embroidered cane dexterously, Lexi can tell he has the weight of the weapon memorized perfectly. **"What fortunate coincidence then... that we happened to run upon one another... I don't believe we've introduced."** He inclines his head in a mocking gesture, grinning out of one side of his mouth. **"Grand Inquisitor Vladimir Von Strogov. Call me Vlad, if you please. And you are...?"** He leaves the question mockingly unfinished in the air, in his thick vampire-noble accent.

Lexi pretends to be impressed by the rather grand introduction. "**Doctor Talia Xass. I'm afraid we haven't had the pleasure yet.**" She gives Vlad a coy smile. "**Though why we never crossed paths before seems beyond me now.**"

**"Indeed. It is a pity that such a vision of beauty has never graced my sight before this day... for I sense, that we have not met here based solely upon happenstance. In fact..."** He turns back and gestures openly at his prisoners, changing the tone of his voice back to severity, **"Bring them out here."** The chain is yanked and the prisoners are ushered forward, forced onto their knees by cruel kicks to the back of their legs. Pretty son all 12 Nighthawks are on their knees in a line. **"In fact, I'd say you would be here to intercept us and try to rescue these dozen dissidents. Correct?"** His gaze returns to Lexi, and a smarmy grin has found its way to Vlad's lips once more, incisors gleaming with malice.

"**Rescue? Who would be foolish enough to try something like that?**" Lexi asks flatly, realizing her ruse was already at an end. Her gaze doesn't leave the Grand Inquisitor even as the Nighthawks are handled into their exposed position before them.

**"Here is the *deal*, Jedi. Listen vell, I vill only say this one time. Unbeknownst to you, your filthy Jedi feet are standing upon our honored Inquisitorial Dueling Arena. It is here, that we Inquisitors sharpen out bodies, so as to strike Terror into tha harts off the greatest Enemies to ever befall the Empire - the hated Jedi. So, you see... Unwittingly, you have already fallen into our trap, at the perfect opportune time and place. Here, you will be utilized as living practice dolls. As fodder to raise the newest crop of Jedi Murderers, who will then spread throughout the galaxy like fungal spores and exterminate the weak, dying Light of the Jedi across the *Entire Universe*."** He spreads his arms and gestures to his 5 Student Inquisitors. Very briefly during his speech, his face flickered way closer than the Inquisitor was from Lexi, after the flicker clears his ugly mug is back where it started, thankfully. During the flicker, however, it seemed to Lexi the lights had gone temporarily dimmer and her surroundings were briefly edged with shadows and gnashing of teeth, howling of wolves, and cawing of ravens. The terror spike passed, however, and everything to her seemed back in its place... for now.

content:
Lexi impassively watches Vlad as he begins his disquisition. His posture and tone reminds her of some of the Masters back on Tython when they were about to get started on a rather long dissertation. The Grand Inquisitor was right to feel confident with the lives of the twelve kneeling before them hanging in the balance. As Vlad summarizes the most recent events surrounding the rescue of Bishop, she's glad Yutra wasn't at that moment standing next to them and that the ex-inquisitor lay low for the past few hours. Even if it was now suspicious that the majority of her Cabal was now suspiciously missing.
For a moment, Lexi contemplates interrupting, but then decides she might be able to learn something in addition to her companions having the time needed to secure their positions after having rushed to intercept the procession. *Patience.* She had rushed here to prevent them from entering the Interrogation suites, where it would be nearly impossible to extricate the squad from the Inquisitor's clutches. If their rescue was to happen, it would be here and rushing the situation when they were outnumbered wasn't to their benefit. Like before they needed to whittle down their numbers. Finally as he moves, she takes a look at the five other Inquisitors.
The largest of the five is a massive armored brute, wearing a featureless vampiric durasteel mask.
The sole female of the group is a Chiss, effecting an air of lethality, she is shapely and tall.
Yellow-eyed and green armored, a thin creepy Inquisitor stands apart from the others, his eyes betraying no hint of a soul beneath.
Another male Inquisitor is studying you, movements deliberate and athletic manner professional. He wears black from head to toe, quality nano-leather evident in his armoring.
The last Inquisitor is unmistakable in his charm and charisma, wearing ornate purple and gold garb, he is clearly alien in origin.

Lexi finishes her examination of the Inquisitors, sizing them up much as she was sure they were her and Zyelle. The Reaper had suprisingly been silent during their encounter with the Inquisitors and she risks glancing over at her companion.
Zyelle has been watching the exchange, but hasn't had an opening to interact just yet, allowing Lexi to take the lead.

**"The facts suggest, Grand Inquisitor... Cabal Sadisticus has gone rogue, or was eliminated, or infiltrated from within."** The comfortable air with which the alien states his conclusion, is underpinned by a waft of pheromones, that make it nearly impossible to disagree with his easy manner of speech.
**"Correct, Inquisitor Nostro. Vot else."**

content: Vlad slowly bends down to one knee, keeping eye contact with Lexi. **"I've been votching you, Jedi. Your eyes seem to have been captured by this particular female. Vot is your name, Mortal? Zess? ... Well, *Zess*, presently... Your life is in *her* hands."** Gestures to the Jedi. Vlad grabs Zess by her hair and pulls her closer, she whimpers crying, chest heaving with hyperventilation. **"Are you paying attention, Zess? Your life... Vot say you, Jedi? Are you going to save poor Zess here?"** Vlad's sharp incisors seem to play over Zess face within inches of striking, then he closes his mouth and seems to force himself to withdraw. **"I'll save you for last, Zess dear."** With his off hand he beckons to his comrade: **"Nostro."**

content: Lexi forces herself to remain calm, and to continue breathing as Vlad pulls Zess closer to him and threatens to kill her then. It takes all her willpower to remain still and not rush in to stop the deathblow. Then he's pulling away and summoning one of his subordinates over with his promise to the poor medic.

Inquisitor Nostro strolls over lazily, cape flowing luxuriously, effecting an air of haughty dexterity. He isn't alone, by his side are two of his personal Gamorrean bodyguards, cultivated genetically to be mountains of muscle by Ul'dan himself, carrying violet sabers, their minds forever open to suggestion by a signature scent of a certain genetic set of pheromones. His tone is that of cold imperial-accent charm: **"Yes, Grand Inquisitor, please... *Allow me*."** Turning to Zess, the alien lets his pheromones waft over Zess' nostrils, causing her eyes to glaze over and turn glassy. Nostro captures her eyes and says pleasantly, **"Greetings, Zess. You shall call me Master. You shall do everything Master says, will you not?"** Zess nods absently. **"*Goooood*... Let's you and I go over here."** He pushes a button on his datapad and Zess cuffs are released. **"Come to me."** Zess comes up to Nostro. **"Look up. Higher. Look up I said. There, stay like that, until Master commands you to move again."** Zess is staring at the ceiling uncomfortably, her jugular exposed. Nostro takes a vibroknife and holds it to Zess neck. **"Are you watching Jedi? If you do anything to displease Grand Inquisitor Vlad, your friend's life ends right here right now."** Zess had gone to the Imperial Academy with Lexi. They had flown together in Thrawn's Final Exam, studied together in the same classes... Yet, here she was, her life on the brink, at the hands of the Cabalite Intellentsia, a ruthless cadre of psychopathic killers, as Inquisitor Yutra had called them... It seems one was more ruthless than the other, and their ruthlessness personalities played against and along with each other's evil tendencies. It was frightening to think of: what would happen if evil comrades actually teamed up and enhanced all of their abilities in a synergistic manner.
Speaking for the first time, Zyelle bites out her words in a dejected voice, **"I think we're in trouble. These guys are *professionals.*"**

Lexi attention goes from Zess' predicament to looking sharply at Zyelle. She hadn't expected the overly confident Reaper to fall into such a dejected state. Her gaze goes back to Vlad and she lets her previously inscrutable expression change to one of realized defeat. "**Yes... Of course. My apologies.**" Reaching out with the Force she touches her thoughts to Zyelle.  ***Trust in the Force,*** she reminds them both.
Inquisitor Nostro bows subtly to Grand Inquisitor Vlad, **"I believe we all understand each other now."**
**"Thank you, Nostro. You are a true disciple. Now... Vot shall ve do, with this Jedi. Any sug-jestions?"**

She had been evidently fighting a deep internal struggle from within: whether she considered herself a Jedi or not. **"I'm... I think... I *am* a Jedi... a different *kind* of Jedi, not that you would know or understand anything about that, all of you being putrid slime-balls lining up to lick Palpatine's buttcrack for another small dollup of power. Think about what would happen if you were to use those brilliantly evil minds for the pursuit of, I dunno, something good - like helping make the world a more beautiful and happy place?"** Zyelle had finished her speech in a loud and arrogant tone.
At the final proclaimation, however, she turned unnaturally cheerful and cheery.

Lexi has been listening to the Inquisitors debate amoungst themselves as to what they were to do with her until the Grand Inquisitor's attention at last turns to Zyelle. The Reaper's proclamation,  causes a small smile to form on her lips, one she quickly hides behind impassivity. The way Zyelle feels, she imagines, is a similar struggle to one Wraith was still coming to terms with since his trial on Dantooine. This place was an ongoing trial for the Reaper and it was still ongoing.

"**I think you're asking too much of them,**" Lexi says as an aside. The Reaper had already begun her insulting tirade of the Inquisitors. "**They've been after all been left here to deal with nothing but the scraps of the Emperor and fulfill the whims of the Reaper Queen.**"

Vlad strides forward, lip quivering with barely controlled boiling rage. **"Very well... Allow me to remind you all, of where you stand."** Dramatically flourishing his cane, he points it at the first Nighthawk in line, Stormtrooper Commander Serpico.

content: **"I have a better idea~!"** All of a sudden, the black-leather clad Inquisitor, Vlad had called him Nath, had taken out his red lightsaber blade and stuck it through Inquisitor Nostro's torso from behind, where his heart would be, a red energy spear plunging out the chest from the surprised Falleen's delicately embroidered gold and purple gown. The Inquisitor's datapad dropped into Nath's hand, who pocketed it instantly, and smoothly having picked up Zess in both hands, Force-leaps onto the stairs between Zyelle and Lexi. This stops the two Gamorreans short, rather confused at this new development, at the foot of the stairs. To Lexi and Zyelle, this Inquisitor Nath has his back turned. He is wearing a black cloak and hood, but Lexi can feel a strangely familiar pull in the Force from his soul. She blinks in disbelief at the realization that this man, he couldn't be *him*... could he?

With the pair of mutated Gamoreans coming towards her and Zyelle, Lexi slowly reaches beneath the lab coat and withdraws her own pair of lightsaber hilts. They had two choices. Surrender, or fight. Then suddenly, the ony called Nath is backstabbing the Falleen and rescuing the enthralled medic. She stares at the dark clad man's back, her lightsabers now in hand. She'd been trying to reach him through the bond for several hours and now... Had he infiltrated the Inquisitors of the Cabal holding his squad captive? The familiar pull makes her question what it was her eyes were seeing so instead she lets the Force guide her. She reaches out to him, looking for Wraith's familiar presence in the man whose soul was pulling her.

The feeling was an odd one, and it was only thanks to his proximity, that Lexi could begin to unravel the mysterious cloak of anonymity that Wraith had cast over himself. He still couldn't look Lexi in the eye, having his back turned. Lexi could sense that he wanted to say much, but was holding himself back. All he could say was: **"Sorry I'm late."**
Zyelle was backing up, cautiously, holding her own saber out but not yet ignited.
**"Lexi who is this man?"**

Vlad had a bewildered expression, for the first time he was ruffled seemingly looking at the ground where his Falleen colleague lay bleeding out back and forth to Nath and back again. Finally standing up, his expression makes an annoyed face: **"You're not Inquisitor Nath."**

content: A small smile spreads across her face as she unwravels the strange presence he had wrapped himself in. Even through the strangeness, she was oddly comforted and reassured. "**No... content: **"Heeeere, piggie piggie..."** He taunts Drok back to the arena behind them.

After a few minutes the two opposing parties are assembled on either side of the arena, each looking down from the walkway bridge surrounding it onto the arena below. Within the arena, on one end stands Drok, snorting loudly and working itself into a rage. On the opposite end, Wraith stands still with his hood drawn up.

Lexi looks out over the arena where hours before they'd won in a melee against Yutra's Cabal. There was still evidence of the fighting that had occured and she wonders sardonically whether by the end of their contest if the arena would be coming out worse. Her lightsabers were at her belt now, her arms crossed over her chest as she watches the Inquisitor's carefully. Ten prisoners were still in their clutches and she doubts very much Vlad was going to take kindly to them making fools of his Cabal.

content: Drok opens the match by charging headlong at the black-clad Reaper, two massive purple saber weapons: a mace and greatsaber are twirling every which way. Wraith is immediately put on the defensive, backflipping and using a single red blade to deflect the attacks on occasion. The Gamorrean is not only strong, but fast, and his furious strikes make mincemeat out of park benches and trees that are in the way. Meanwhile, Wraith is being agile in his retreat, waiting for the pig-like alien to wear himself out.

After Drok backs Wraith up against the wall of the Arena, Wraith is seemingly trapped and looks back and forth for some way to escape the looming Super-Reaper. Drok chuckles evily, breathing hard and flourishing his weapons in eagerness.

Drok charges in, greatsaber leading. Wraith uses his red saber to block, swiping away the first attack, but can't block the second weapon without losing his own, as the mace crashes into his lightsaber with great force. Wraith's red lightsaber goes flying away and lands in the grass, harmlessly deactivating. Wraith, weaponless, is backed into a corner.
**"HROINK~SQUEEEEE~"**
Drok, seeing his opponent disarmed, goes in for the kill, lunging both weapons at where Wraith is standing!

Lexi watches the match with interest. Wraith keeping up the defensive was leading the Gamorean on, allowing his opponent to tire. Neither scored any hits during the exchange. She peers down as Wraith suddenly finds himself trapped, and weaponless. Rather than being concerned she continues to watch knowing her partner must have some trick up his sleeve.

At the final moment of the fight, Drok's face lights up with triumph at his imminent victory. Both of Drok's sabers skewer Wraith's black cloak, going through and puncturing Wraith's torso. However, there's just one problem... Wraith is no longer there. Instead, having discarded the cloak, he had disappeared in a smooth trick of legerdemain. Wraith races up Drok's massive forearms, stepping on the Gamorrean's shoulders and taking an aqua-green swipe with his lifesaber towards the pigman's head. Doing a forward flip, Wraith lands in the middle of the arena, not bothering to look at his opponent behind him. Enraged, Drok turns around. Then... the top third of Drok's head seems to slide off slowly, just above the eyes, leaving a nice anatomical cross section of a Gamorrean's brain. Drok pitches into the dirt with a crash that reverberates the arena.

Lexi watches with facination as Wraith excecutes his attack, leaving behind the nearly decapitated Reaper in his wake. She looks up at Vlad, waiting for the consequence of Wraith so easily overcomming the supposedly superior Super Reaper created by Dr Ul'dan.

**"Hmph. Don't look so proud of yourself... After ve're done vith you three, Ul'dan vill just replace Drok vith another clone. You are a powerful combatant, hoo-ever you are. Vell, at least, ve know you von't be using that trick tvice. Thanks for that little bit of intel, I don't think you're faster than Mantis, so... you are still doomed"** He turns to his men: **"That's enough playing with our food. Make ready to finish them off."** Vlad turns back to Wraith: **"Let's see how you fare against all 5 of us... Wraith."**

content: Wraith calmly takes a defensive stance, widening his feet, lowering his center of gravity and raising his blade next to his ear gripping it with both hands, eyes meeting Vlad's calmly.

As the other Inquisitors begin to gather to confront Wraith, Lexi drops into the arena next to him. Reaching out to Veq she gives him the signal that the time had come. "**You forget.. he's not alone. And I didn't come alone either.**"
There is a thumping of feet and leaping off the edge is a heavy version of Veq. Crashing into the arena, Wraith is clearly surprised at the newcomer.
Floating down on unseen ethereal winds, hands outspread with palms up, twin flames burning with fire, is the firewitch Yutra. Wraith is likewise taken aback at this newest appearance.
Catching Wraith's suprise she gives him a small smile. "**I've been busy making friends,**" she says with a small shrug.
Telepathically imparts: ***"Greetings, Jedi Wraith. I am called Veq. I have sworn to defend Jedi Lexi with my life. It's good to finally meet you."***
**"Uh, hi there. Name's Yutra. I hope you don't think we're going to do all the work for you..."**
Lexi realizes there's still one of their number missing. "**Is Zye going to miss the party?**"

Zyelle had been shy to approach Wraith, having been blushing a deep pink, at having had her tender emotional reveal as a Jedi be upstaged by the Return of Wraith. She also was somewhat awestruck at the Reaper's gracefullness and courage. Upon hearing her name mentioned, Zyelle jumps nimbly down into the arena, standing up from a crouch and strutting over to Wraith, putting on her sexiest come-hither eyes. **"We've already met. Lexi talks in glowing terms about you, *Wraith*."**

In response to the new introductions, Wraith turns and regards each of Lexi's new team members with an appraising look. Upon the re-introduction of Zyelle, he once again lays his eyes upon the sexy Reaper and there is definitely some kind of energy sparking between them. Zyelle had said that she had rebuffed Wraith's advances, but her strange attitute change didn't seem to quite jive with her proclamations earlier to Lexi. Wraith nods slowly, **"Good to see you all. We're all going to have to work together if we want to come out alive."**
**"My name is Wraith. I was once a Reaper, but I've left that lifestyle behind in pursuit of my Quest for Truth."**
Zyelle had gotten serious, losing her childish charm, and becoming focused on Wraith: **"It seems you and I have something in common..."**

Wraith had removed the rest of his Inquisitor's garbs and had re-summonned his Spirit Armor. **"I'm sorry, but it's not something I could have shared openly the last time we met, Lady of the Falls."**
**"Hmph. I have secrets too you know, I hope you don't think you know everything there is to know about me, either."**
**"I'm certain you have your own secrets too, Lady. However... If you wish to remain mysterious, those clothes aren't helping."**
Zyelle blushes, covering up her face with her hands, and turning away from Wraith, walking away.
Looking at Zyelle's retreating form, Yutra smirks at Wriath:**"Hmmmm... Why do I get the feeling you're going to break my heart too."**
**"Forgive me, but we've not met."**
**"Ex-Inquisitor Yutra, but you can call me whatever you like, handsome."**
**"Lexi, you're going to have to give me the full story someday..."**

"**You seem to be popular today,**" Lexi says with a great deal of mirth as her female companions literally seem to swoon in her lover's presence.  She had the feeling though that Zyelle hadn't been completely truthful regarding her first meeting with Wraith, but the story would have to be told another day. "**Someday, perhaps,**" she says non committedly.
**"I'm afraid we have bigger problems. Is your friend going to be alright?"**
Zyelle had stopped and faced away from the group, a few paces away.
**"Vell vell vell, Inquisitor Yutra. So *you* are the traitor in our midst."**

content: The Insectoid Inquisitor had been chattering his teeth in anticipation, saliva running down his neck. As he saw the two soft targets, he figured his part of the battle would be over with quickly.

Bending his knees unnaturally with an insectile squeak, he weaved a zigzag attempting to remove both Lexi's and Zyelle's head from their shoulders. However, unexpectedly, he was met with Lexi's yellow and green blade both times. The verdant green of Lexi's blade was nothing like Mantis' poisonous green emanation. Lexi had learned at the Jedi Academy that Sith sabers could only be red, but clearly their depth of malice was reflected in the khyber crystal they selected. Even though both combatants had green blades, they were green for entirely different reasons, Lexi knew.

As the insectoid Inquisitor charges in, her attempts to thwart his progress with a well timed Force Slam doesn't come to fruition as she finds herself on the defensive protecting not only her own neck, but Zyelle's. Her yellow blade easily bats aside the stroke aimed for her and with her connection to the Force her blade finds his second attack before it can come close to Zyelle. Once again she places herself in front of the exposed Reaper while she pulls herself together. ***Come on, Zyelle,*** she pleads, urging her to come to her senses.

Struggling against the onslaught of the alien Inquisitor,  Lexi manages to push him away and with the physical effort she also sends a mental push in the Force towards Zyelle. ***Snap out of it.*** It was perhaps unkind the force  in which she pushed, but with the deadly mantis in front of them, Zyelle was vulnerable.

Yutra was levitating and conjured two Enmitic Fireballs, one in each hand - sending both at the Chiss Inquisitor, she was surprised to see two clear perfectly reflective floating shards of glass appear and swallow the fireballs away into some other, crystalline dimension, detonating harmlessly on that plane of existence.

The Chiss Inquisitor had been likewise flying by standing atop a levitating shard of glass. Moving her ands to kinetically control the crystalline shards, she summoned more seemingly from thin air and unleashed a Rain of Glass Shards, meant to turn Yutra into Swiss Cheese.

Fortunately, Yutra's ruse worked, and one of the Fireballs returned from the mirror, being controlled and guided by Yutra's hatred of her fellow Inquisitors. It crashed into Luna from behind, surprising her and leaving a black spot of charred flesh. Additionally, Yutra had summoned a Flamethrower of Hate from her hands, burning and blowing away any glass shards that came near to skewering her own grey flesh.

Zyelle received a telepathic message from Lexi and this brought her out of her own internal thoughts. She hadn't even realized that her life was in danger, until a poisonous green blade had come within centimeters of ending her existence. Only Lexi's skilled bladework managed to turn away the Assassin's Mantis Blade.

Angry at herself, for letting her guard down, she shook her head back and forth, opening her eyes and activating her Cybernetic Third Eye. Tracking the movements of the mantis-like Inquisitor more easily, she took off in a massive gust of wind. Catching up, she ran alongside the bug-man, and they clashed swords on the go. Zyelle was a Reaper, however, and her movements were several steps ahead of the Insectoid Inquisitor. Green bug-fluid squirted out in his wake, as Zyelle managed to use her cyan blade to easily dissect two of his extra limbs, insectoid vestigial stick-arms flying away in the way of their battle. Mantis, forced to retreat, betrayed no hint of pain or damage, beyond two tubules pumping disgusting bug fluid out of his torso like two small sewage pipes.
**"What's a matter? Don't like it when your prey fights back huh?"**

Vlad hovers, flapping his bat wings, above the combat. He can't quite believe it, but the Jedi and her newly arrived companions, were actually winning. For the first time, in his black heart, he began to feel something he usually only dished out to others - *fear*. This once again stopped him from being able to enact the highly complicated Black Spell that would typically allow his team to win any prolonged team encounters. Vlad bit his lip, blood running down in a trickle down his chin.

Attempting to enact this Sith Sorcery required an uninterrupted amount of focus and channeling, as well as a clear area of impact. Below him, the arena filled to the brim with all manner of battling combatants was the perfect target. Focusing his mind, using all of his hatred against this plucky young group of goodie-two-shoes... He felt the words of the Spell welling up in his mind, as though it were a black liquid sloshing and frothing to the limits of its containment. In a final shout of Sith words long lost and forgotten to all but a select few Dark Side practitioners, the container burst, and he finally unleashed his penultimate spell: **"Now... You shall experience... True Fear!"** He had to bite his own tongue off, in order to swallow enough Vital Fluid in the form of his own blood to make this Sorcery manifest forcibly. Nevertheless, it would be well worth it, if it ended his opposition permanently. Vlad could palpably feel the Souls of his soon-to-be victims attempt to Resist his True Fear Spell within the strands of the Force, as if somehow realizing that this would be a devastating attack on their very Souls. content: Lexi's world suddenly shrinks, darkness creeping in from the corners of her vision to resolve her whole world into pitch black darkness.

The sound of flying rodents, or bats, approaches from somewhere far away, and she feels herself swept up by a legion of bats, their loud supersonic screeching nearly driving her mad. Forced to cover her eyes and ears, she feels the furry wings of vampire bats as they play over her whole body.

Then, just as suddenly, the bat swarm disappears, and Lexi is left standing alone, in a dark haunted forest, twisted trees in every direction, except one - a small game trail heading for a small tavern ahead, circled by wooden palisades and torches - the sole source of light in any visible direction.
To Lexi's amazement, her lightsabers have turned into longswords, and her Spirit Robe design has shifted also, to something more befitting antiquity.
For a brief instant, Lexi feels as if she is in a story book, then fleeting sensation passes...

After a few paces, she begins to resolve the details of the tavern. It is built using some of the same haunted wood as the rest of the forest, ugly and twisted, which doesn't help the forboding atmosphere any.

The foreboding forest she finds herself leaves her looking around in disbelief. With her connection to the Verdant Force, she's quickly able to discern she is trapped in a Dreamscape, mush as Binoc had found himself in. Knowing the nightmare was mostly in her mind she realizes was half the battle, she still needs to escape. Seeing the tavern ahead she starts in ahead, watching the woods around her warily as she walks down the lone game trail.
A not-so-distant howl is heard, and a pack of hungry snarling and drooling mangy wolves jump out of the forest onto the path ahead, blocking her way to the tavern.
The half dozen feral dire wolves have ugly yellow eyes and look half-starved in the barely illuminated dim light of the moon overhead.

Lexi charges in with nimble speed, before the wolves can organize any form of attack, and cuts down two right away. She is rewarded by a bite on her soft, delicate calf muscle. The Jedi feels the rabies attempt to enter her bloodstream, and uses a modicum of Force energy to burn it away inside her arteries before it can reach her heart and infect her whole circulation.

Of the remaining wolves, she manages to decapitate one and slash dead another. The last two wolves look at each other sharing a fearful expression. The two remaining predators retreat cautiously into the darkness, their yellow eyes the only thing visible... and then they dimly disappear too. Lexi is left alone once more.
Entering the Tavern, she sees it abandonned save for a solitary hooded cloaked figure sitting at the bar facing away from her. He looks shapely and familiar to her senses.
Lexi slowly approaches the bar, keeping at arms length of the man. While her senses tell her the cloaked man is familiar to her, she hasn't discerned if he's friend or foe.

A familiar voice intones: **"You made it. Are you alright? Have a seat and drink, it looks like you had to fight off the local predators too..."** Turning around, you see stuble and long hair belonging to Wraith, although he is also dressed in the garbs of antiquity.

While they pin up their notice, Lexi takes a look at the array of notices herself.

The first poster reads: *Be wary all yee who wander these lands, creatures of unholy dark magiks stalk the woods. Fearful ye shall be, for if thoust are overpowered your Body and Soul shall be dragged to the dungeons of Count Vlad, and your blood shall be drank daily from your veins as the vampyre and his minions make a drink out of your life's very essence... Yee have been warned.*

The second poster reads: *The Cruel Witch Lunestara has bewitched a coven of Holy Sisters, turning them from the Light of Goodness to the Worship of the Red-Eyed Devil. Be wary of their Bewitching Chant. Whatever you do, do not look into her mirror, or yee soul too shall become bewitched, lost forever to Satan. May God have Mercy on Their Wicked Souls.*

The third poster reads: *The fearsome Man-Boar has been spotted by farmers on no less than 17 occasions since harvest. It stands 3 meters tall, with glowing malignant yellow eyes, massive tusks, and mangy tuft of reddish brown blood-slicked hair. The beast bears massive muscular limbs, and can walk upright on two short, stubby cloven feet. It wields a cruel, rusty meat cleaver and wears a half-torn butcher's apron, for some unknown reason. At this point, the cattle lost to its appetite is beyond count. Farmer Sam says that he knows where the beast makes its lair: a bone-filled burned out basement of the former Manor of Lord Drikhar.*
Lexi reads over the notices with a frown. "**Man-Boar or Witch first? I think we should place ours next to our first destination.**"
She looks on the board to see if there's a map of some sort.
There is an interesting depiction of the Man-Boar underneath his posting.
Looking at the available map, she memorizes their location in relation to their objectives and gauges which of the two is the closest.
Lexi sees that the Coven and Ruins are equidistant from the Village, and the Castle is the farthest away in the woods.
"**Well... let's start with the Coven,**" she says and takes their notice to put near the one for the Witch.

content: The duo leaves the confines of the tavern and see that just a little bit down the road, is a small village bearing about a dozen medieval buildings all in all. The creaky sign pointing towards it, reads: *Fernstead*.
Outside, it remains perpetually nighttime, with a prominent large full moon in the sky that illuminates everything in a grey, ethereal haze.

The village seems abandonned, as the duo strolls into the village, a small amount of ground-fog conceals their feet all the way up to the ankles. A few ravens caw in the distance, their heads on a swivel and turning their large, black pupillary orbs at the two strangers entering their midst. Cawing, a few take off and fly away.
Far in the distance, in the thick deep forested wood, can be gleamed a massive structure. It is a fully realized Medieval Fortified Castle, hauntingly backlit by the oversized full moon.

Lexi cranes her neck to look one way then another as she surveys the seemingly abandoned village. Her attention comes to the ravens as they walk through the avenue. "**How much control does Vlad has over this place?**" she wonders aloud. "**It is a dream after all.**"

Wraith eyes the black predator-birds wearily. **"Vlad is a visitor here, just like us, but his presence may be enhanced by the environment created within this Dreamscape, since he is it's ultimate creator. He has subconsciously crafted the rules of this Dream World to his mental specifications. We don't know the rules exactly, but he doesn't either - not on a conscious level. However, his instincts will give him a great advantage over us. In order to defeat him, we have to outwit him. For example, this scene is likely a representation of his subconscious, manifested into reality. We entered his Dream Reality and now we have to play by his rules. In order to win, we have to take on roles, within any scene. *Winning* roles."**

content: Lexi nods her gaze following his before looking back at the abandoned town. "**He's created a world where everyone is his prey and darkness has taken root.**" Looking up at the night sky with the solitary moon she adds, "**A perpetual night.**"

In the distance, bells begin to toll. They appear to be coming from the North, where the map had located the Chapel-Seminary, AKA the Nunnery. Strolling through the village, the duo is stopped at the very center of Fernstead, where all its little roads cross and leave a muddy flattened puddle-area filled with animal track-prints. Standing in the middle of the rained, muddy town square, is a singular lone figure: a rider atop a horse, wearing a jack-o-lantern on his head, or maybe that *was* his head. The eyes glowed a malignant green, and green fire burned out of the top. The horse is massive, black, and clearly a Nightmare - a type of undead horse billowing purple fire in place of a mane. The Nightmare whinnies, rearing its forward legs, and the Pumpkin-Head laughs wickedly. In both hands, you see he wields a farmer's full length scythe, cruel and rusted. It makes scything noises as he seems to swing it by your heads, dashing by on his steed. Echoing, otherworldly masculine 
laughter follows in his wake.

Wraith is taken by surprise, as they round the corner into the hands of a scythe-wielding maniac on a horse. A metallic swoosh is all he hears as the weapon's sharp edge comes in at him cutting through the fog like a knife, slicing the front of Wraith's torso, nearly missing his heart but managing to dissect and puncture Wraith's left lung with ease. A long, nasty wicked looking gash is left in the wake of the attack, pumping out Wraith's precious blood like a red, gushing fountain.

With a fluid motion, she unsheathes her sword, its blade humming with a soft, golden light. The air crackles with anticipation, the only sound besides the Nightmare's snorts, and the Pumpkin-Head's chilling laughter.

The creature charges, a whirlwind of hooves and fire. Lexi meets the onslaught with a whirlwind of her own, her sword a blur of motion. She parries the scythe's deadly arc, the metal ringing against metal, sparks flying. With a twist of her wrist, she ripostes, the golden blade finding its mark in the Pumpkin-Head's chest. The creature shudders, the fire around it flickering and dying, and then collapses with a sickening thud.

content:
Lexi isn't quite satisfied by his reasoning, the wound looking to have been much worse than a normal infection. Her protests are cut off by the tolling of to convent bells, each one sending a shiver of progressing intensity through her. 
When the nuns swarm the nightmare, she draws one of her blades and swipes at them to keep their weapons away from them.
The Vampire nuns are dispatched by a combination of swipes from both Jedi and purple flames spurting from the Nightmare's Nostrils.

At the entrance to the Church, a singular Nun stands, her curves hauntingly familiar to both Jedi. Evil chanting is heard emanating from her lips, and Black Fireballs fly towards the Nightmare, which hops out of the way at the last second, legs uncoiling just as the trio of detonations leave the ground looking like a mortar strike just landed. Yutra's eyes glow an evil red, similar to when Lexi had first met the Inquisitor.

Lexi closes her eyes and calls on her Verdant Sight, to examine the familiar Nun: a shadowy black/purple tendril is attached to Yutra at the back of the neck, and snakes its way up to the roof, where the Bell is mounted. Following its trail, Lexi spots the Source. Invisible to the naked eye, but visible in Verdant Vision, the person ringing the bell in the gantry is seen only to you: a shorthaired blue-skinned Witch using some kind of magic to stay invisible to ordinary vision.
**"I thought that Inquisitor was on our side... what gives?"**

The wood holding the bell cracks as Lexi succeeds in ripping it from its embrace. Luna, caught off guard, cries out in pain, her invisibility shield flickering and fading as the bell collides with her small frame. Both bell and Witch make the journey to the ground.

The bell had clanged against the head of the Witch with a sickening thud. As both fall down, the Witch screamed a wicked scream as she fell - the shriek ended in a sickening glurtch as Lunestara gets crushed like a pancake, a single wrist reaching out weakly from beneath the bronze hunk, only to fall wilting. A puddle of red blood begins to slowly fill in the muddy depression left behind. A few seconds pass, as rain pours and all noise of battle stops. Looking up at the sky, rainfall hits Yutra's face, washing away some of her mascara. Stumbling, Yutra leans against the doorway clutching her head, trying to shake off the last effects of the bewitching compulsion. **"I-- Where am I? Lexi? I feel ran over by a Ronto..."** At last, straightening up, she slowly walks over to the duo on horseback, making a circle and gawking at the massive oversized beast. Finally, looking up she smiles: **"Room for one more?"** The muscular oversized deathhorse had plenty of room for the slender Ex-Inquisitor. Shrugging, Wraith reached his vambrace down to grasp Yutra at the forearm and nimbly swings her up to join them atop the Nightmare. Exhaling and fixing her nun's dress Yutra huffs breathily: **"Sorry for the friendly fire, but I've got a present to make up for my being naughty."** Reaching into her bra, she withdraws a small pouch in hand. She jiggles the pouch in front of their faces, inside is some kind of crystal container, audibly sloshing a liquid within. **"Holy Water. Bane to Vampyres. Stops regeneration."**
**"Where you guys headed?"**

"**Yutra!**" Lexi exclaims with relief that the compulsion had ended with the death of the Witch. She waits until Wraith has hauled her up onto the Nightmare and turns on the beast's back to look at her. "**There's some sort of Man-Boar we need to confront at some ruins before we can head to Vlad's castle.**" She eyes the pouch. "**Keep that safe. We'll need it when go to confront him.**"

Points to the massive castle backlit by the full moon. **"That's one option, or we could go straight to the heart of darkness. The boar might just be a waste of time and fighting spirit on our part."**

content: "**If Yutra was here, it's possible Veq or Zyelle may be at the ruins. If we skip investigating the ruins, would their spell be broken as well?**

**"In theory yes, but I didn't feel them in the dreamscape with the Force when I reached out earlier, maybe they're still on the outside."** Riding closer to the Vlad's Castle, swarms of vampire bats screech overhead in rabid excitement. You can hear their little teeth chittering with hunger and have to knock them off your necks on occasion.
**"Nice place, lets crash it."**

Battling away the swarm of bats seems to be the least of their concerns as the castle looms ahead of them. "**Then let's not waste anymore time,** she says, in agreement with her companions' assertions.
**"Agreed."**
The trio make their way on horseback atop the Nightmare to Count Vlad's Castle. It is dark and slightly rainy as they have to halt their horse a mile away to plan their assault.
**"What do you think? Scale the walls or fly in from above?"**

**"I'll climb atop the wall and you teleport to my location so we're all together. Then Yutra can swoop in once I give the all clear signal for the both of you."**

content: Lexi eyes the skeletons warily, remembering how they've run into reanimated corpses in the past. She moves with Wraith as he heads towards the door and peaks out into the next room once it's opened.
Area V
Beyond is the inner courtyard of Vlad's Castle, with red carpets leading up to his personal keep and, presumably, Throne Room within.
"**Time to announce ourselves?**" Lexi stares up the carpeted avenue to Vlad's domain after looking around at the presumably deserted castle.

As the trio snake their way amongst the tall, gothic halls and approach the Doorway to Vlad's Domain, a singular bulky figure steps out to stand in their way. A massive barely restrained enraged brutish Man-Boar, wielding a cleaver and nostrils fuming.
"**Well, well...**" Lexi eyes the man-boar as it reveals itself. "**Looks like we have to fight it anyways.**" Drawing her two blades she takes up a defensive stance.
**"~HREEEEEE~!"**
Brandishing his own weapon in a dexterous arc, Wraith takes up a fighting stance too: **"Why can't we fight some more hot witches instead?"**

content: **"How do you like your steak cooked..."**
Summoning up Enmitic Oil the substance begins to shimmer and coalesce into a pre-fireball.

The massive man-boar swings its rusty cleaver thrice at the girls, and attempts to gore Wraith with its tusks. Nimbly clashing the tusks against his own sword, Wraith grits his teeth and with a burst of Force power unleashes two cuts across both eyes: forming double 'X's and blinding the Man-Boar, as it squeals in pain monstrously, its humid rotting breath palpable in the air.

The Man-Boar roars, a guttural sound that echoes through the courtyard, and lunges at Lexi, its cleaver aimed at her head. Lexi, her movements swift and precise, parries the blow with her glowing green sword, the force of the impact sending a jolt through her arms. Before the Man-Boar can recover, she spins, her golden blade flashing in the dim light, and plunges it deep into the creature's chest.

As if to accentuate the scene, two fireballs explode behind the boar (Yutra missing) and backlight the scene of the boar yelling his death scream in pain monstrously - blade sticking out the center chest. It topples, blade withdrawn by Lexi's wrist nimbly, and rolls down the stairs, out of sight. The massive doors to Vlad's Inner Sanctum now loom.
Lexi doesn't wait for her companions as she strides towards the doors.
The doors creak open on their own hauntingly, beyond a throne room looms atop which sits the Master of the Castle himself. **"How konsiderate of you. To valk directly into my trap."**
**"Time to end this little farce, by ending you."**

content: Vlad flies forth on his leathery wings, half bouyed by unseen ethereal ghostly strings. His Cruel Sword finds its mark instinctively, as if hunting for blood. It pierces both Wraith's Heart from behind and Yutra's Heart from the front, before the two can even blink - they were both on a Death Timer. Lexi had eaten two swipes from the Cruel Vampiric Sword, badly cutting her with its razor-sharp edge.

Turning around for another lethal pass, he scores another hit on Lexi's exposed flesh, but in turn Lexi is able to read his tradjectory correctly and end his life with her own Green Verdant Blade. Vlad's eyes go wide as the Holy Water begins unraveling his form. **"N-noo! Impossible---!"** He turns to cinders, as if made of cigarette ash.
The entire world cracks in half, as if a shard of glass split in half.
